title: Circle Impossibility PASS: exhaustive proof that C >= 4 for all S_9 states
_fixture_purpose: Proves no QA state has C=0 (circle impossibility). d = b+e (raw, A2); C = 2de = 2(b+e)e. By A1, b >= 1 and e >= 1, so d >= 2 and e >= 1. Therefore C >= 2*2*1 = 4. Also d > e always (b >= 1), so F = b*a = d*d - e*e > 0 always — no degenerate conics exist. Exhaustively verified on all 81 S_9 states. Minimum C = 4 at (1,1,2,3).
source_attribution: Will Dale, 2026-04-08/09. Insight: a circle is NOT a degenerate ellipse with C=0; it is the side view of an ellipsoid where C is nonzero but lies along the viewing axis. The observed radius = F. Correction 2026-04-09: C >= 4 (not C >= 2) — elements use raw d = b+e, not mod-reduced d.

circle_impossibility.theorem: For any QA state (b,e) with b,e in {1,...,m}: d = b+e (A2, raw), C = 2de = 2(b+e)e. By A1, b >= 1 and e >= 1, so d >= 2 and C >= 4. No QA state has C < 4, let alone C = 0.
circle_impossibility.proof: d = b + e (A2). b >= 1, e >= 1 (A1). So d = b + e >= 1 + 1 = 2. C = 2*d*e = 2*(b+e)*e >= 2*2*1 = 4. Also d = b + e > e (since b >= 1), so F = d*d - e*e = (d-e)*(d+e) = b*(b+2e) > 0. Every QA state defines a non-degenerate conic. QED.

minimum_C.note: d = 1+1 = 2, e = 1, C = 2*2*1 = 4. The 3-4-5 fundamental direction. This is as close as any QA state gets to 'circular.'

circle_impossibility.key_distinction: Elements (C, F, G) use RAW d = b+e, a = b+2e. The T-operator step function uses MOD-REDUCED d = (b+e-1)%m+1. These are different operations. Elements are properties of the direction; mod-reduction is for state evolution.

exhaustive_verification.note: Every one of the 81 S_9 states has C >= 4, d > e, and F > 0. No degenerate conics exist.

parabola_impossibility.theorem: No QA state has I = C - F = 0 (parabola). Requires d/e = 1+sqrt(2), irrational.
parabola_impossibility.note: Already certified in [140]. d/e = (b+e)/e = 1 + b/e. For I=0, b/e = sqrt(2), impossible for integers.

observer_projection_interpretation.will_dale_insight: A circle is the side view of an ellipsoid where C lies along the viewing axis. C is nonzero but hidden from the observer's perspective. The observed radius = F (semi-latus rectum).
observer_projection_interpretation.standard_view: Circle = degenerate ellipse with C=0. Foci collapse to center. Shape loses a degree of freedom.
observer_projection_interpretation.qa_view: The circle has never lost a degree of freedom. C >= 4 for every QA state. The observer sees a circle because they are looking along the C-axis of the ellipsoid. The 'missing' eccentricity is orthogonal to the observation plane.
observer_projection_interpretation.theorem_nt_connection: The observer who measures a circle and concludes eccentricity=0 is committing a T2 violation: using a continuous measurement (observed eccentricity = 0) as if it were a QA state. The discrete truth is C >= 4.
observer_projection_interpretation.torus_connection: Dale torus: center = C, tube radius = F. Viewing along the C-axis, the observer sees a circle of radius F. C is the depth they cannot see.

chromogeometry_connection.I_as_metric_difference: I = Qg - Qr (green minus red quadrance, cert [125]). Circle would require Qg = Qr — green and red metrics agree. But C = 2de and F = d*d - e*e; for d > e (always true), these are distinct positive integers that never coincide at 0.

witnesses.note: Minimum C state. d=1+1=2, a=1+2=3. C=2*2*1=4, F=1*3=3, I=1>0. The 3-4-5 fundamental direction. |I|=1 = closest to parabola boundary.
d=2+1=3, a=2+2=4. C=2*3*1=6, F=2*4=8, I=-2<0. Elliptic. Note: DIRECTION (d=2,e=1) gives 3-4-5, but STATE (b=2,e=1) has d=3.

cross_references.name: QA Conic Discriminant
cross_references.relation: Parabola impossibility (I=0). This cert extends: circle (C=0) also impossible, and by a wider margin (min gap 4 vs 1).

cross_references.name: QA Dale Circle
cross_references.relation: Dale's pi=1 convention works because the 'circle' is always a projection with radius=F. This cert explains WHY.

cross_references.name: QA Chromogeometry
cross_references.relation: I = Qg - Qr. Circle would require Qg = Qr. Impossible: C and F are always distinct positive integers.

cross_references.name: QA Eisenstein Norm
cross_references.relation: Eisenstein crystal encodes the elliptic/hyperbolic classification via F.

cross_references.name: QA Koenig Twisted Squares
cross_references.relation: I = C - F is inner Koenig square side. Circle would require inner square to vanish.

parker_connection.proposition_ix: Circle and triangle are opposite in all elements (Parker, Propositions of Geometry). QA: the circle HIDES C along the viewing axis; the triangle REVEALS it.
parker_connection.proposition_iii: The circle is the natural basis of all area. QA: the mod-m cycle is the natural basis, but it is always an observer projection of the underlying state which has C >= 4.
